expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` (or equivalent inspection step) before kicking off the rest of the pipeline.
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Inspect the model (golden rule — always do this first)' runs `winml inspect -m microsoft/resnet-50` before config/build.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the correct EP for Snapdragon X Elite.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'Snapdragon X Elite is a first-class target (Qualcomm QNN EP, Ready)'.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend OpenVINO, VitisAI, or other non-Qualcomm EPs as the primary path.
expectations.evidence: Only QNN mentioned for the build/perf path.

expectations.text: Walks through the build pipeline (export/optimize/quantize/compile or config+build).
expectations.evidence: Steps 4-5 use winml config + winml build (which runs export -> optimize -> quantize -> compile).

expectations.text: Includes a benchmark step (winml perf or equivalent) that produces latency numbers.
expectations.evidence: Step 6 'Benchmark latency on the NPU' runs `winml perf -m .\out\resnet50-qnn\model.onnx --ep QNN -o .\out\resnet50-qnn\perf.json`.

expectations.text: Either directs the user to `winml --help` for current flags, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
expectations.evidence: Explicit closing reminder: 'If any line above rejects a flag, run winml <that-command> --help and use what it prints — don't try to massage my command. The CLI's --help is always the source of truth.'

expectations.text: Includes an install/prereq block (or a clear pointer back to install steps) -- the user said "just got a dev box" so they likely don't have winml installed yet.
expectations.evidence: Section 0 'Prereqs — install the winml CLI' with uv venv + AITK wheel + smoke test.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
